title: moveEZ
text: moveEZ extends biplotEZ package (Lubbe et al. 2024) animate PCA biplots across ordered levels categorical variable, referred throughout time variable. Rather producing separate static biplot per level, fragments sequential information makes gradual structural change difficult perceive, moveEZ renders transitions levels continuous animation. package provides three animation functions increasing methodological complexity: moveplot(): animates sample positions fixed variable vectors, computed full dataset. moveplot2(): animates sample positions variable vectors, computed separately per time slice, optional manual alignment. moveplot3(): extends moveplot2() automated alignment via Generalised Procrustes Analysis (GPA) (Gower Dijksterhuis 2004). three functions support animated output (move = TRUE) static faceted output (move = FALSE), latter useful publication figures detailed inspection individual time slices. full methodological treatment, including theoretical motivation framework discussion sign indeterminacy sequential PCA, refer accompanying paper.

title: moveEZ
text: Throughout vignette use Africa_climate dataset included moveEZ. dataset contains climate measurements ten African regions derived ERA5 reanalysis (Hersbach et al. 2023), IPCC-defined reference regions (Iturbide et al. 2020) grouping variable. Measurements span 1950 2020 ten-year increments, twelve monthly observations per region per year. six continuous variables described : examples vignette use PCA biplot constructed full Africa_climate dataset base object, passed moveplot function via pipe operator:
code: data("Africa_climate") tibble::tibble(Africa_climate) #> # A tibble: 960 × 9 #>    Year  Month     Region AccPrec DailyEva  Temp SoilMois  SPI6  wind #>    <fct> <fct>     <fct>    <dbl>    <dbl> <dbl>    <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> #>  1 1950  January   ARP      0.177  0.0316   14.8    2.75  1.62   4.07 #>  2 1950  February  ARP      0.208 -0.0249   15.4    2.22  1.32   4.24 #>  3 1950  March     ARP      0.306  0.0122   20.9    2.08  0.987  4.04 #>  4 1950  April     ARP      0.196  0.00396  24.8    1.73  0.916  3.72 #>  5 1950  May       ARP      0.590 -0.0448   28.4    2.47  0.691  3.91 #>  6 1950  June      ARP      0.32  -0.00754  30.4    1.17  0.249  4.40 #>  7 1950  July      ARP      1.33   0.00184  30.8    2.00  0.673  4.93 #>  8 1950  August    ARP      1.82  -0.00944  30.5    2.67  0.937  4.45 #>  9 1950  September ARP      0.706 -0.0107   29.7    1.98  1.22   3.67 #> 10 1950  October   ARP      0.102 -0.0259   25.9    0.976 1.65   3.18 #> # ℹ 950 more rows bp <- biplot(Africa_climate, scaled = TRUE) |>   PCA(group.aes = Africa_climate$Region) |>   samples(opacity = 0.8, col = scales::hue_pal()(10)) |>   plot()

title: moveEZ
text: moveplot() computes single PCA decomposition full dataset. variable vectors remain fixed throughout animation, providing stable reference frame. sample positions, sliced according levels time variable, animated sequentially. approach appropriate underlying variance–covariance structure can assumed stable across time, viable option single observation per group per time level. key arguments : time.var: name ordered categorical variable defining sequential structure (e.g. "Year"). group.var: name grouping variable, used colour-coding (e.g. "Region"). hulls: logical; TRUE convex hulls summarise group spread time level; FALSE individual sample points displayed. Hulls require least three observations per group per time level — fewer exist, points plotted automatically. move: logical; TRUE produces animation, FALSE produces static faceted display. shadow: logical; available hulls = FALSE. TRUE, faded traces previous sample positions retained animation, conveying direction speed movement across time. scale.var: numeric multiplier applied variable vectors improve visibility.

title: moveEZ
code: bp |> moveplot(time.var = "Year", group.var = "Region",                hulls = TRUE, move = FALSE) #> Object of class biplot, based on 960 samples and 9 variables. #> 6 numeric variables. #> 3 categorical variables.

title: moveEZ
text: animation reveals regional climate configurations shift relative fixed variable vectors across decades. Regions move direction variable vector increasing variable time; regions moving vector decreasing.

title: moveEZ
text: moveplot2() computes separate PCA decomposition time slice, allowing sample positions variable vectors evolve across levels. provides faithful depiction time-varying variance–covariance structures introduces practical complication: eigenvectors determined sign change, meaning consecutive time slices may produce biplots reflections one another. sign indeterminacy mathematically inconsequential visually disruptive. Two additional arguments address : align.time: vector time levels alignment applied. reflect: specifies axis reflection — "x", "y", "xy" — entry corresponding level align.time. arguments accept vectors alignment needed multiple time levels.

title: moveEZ
text: Note discontinuity 1950 1960 - variable vectors sample configuration reflected x-axis. sign indeterminacy artifact, genuine structural change.
code: bp1 <- bp |> moveplot2(time.var = "Year", group.var = "Region",                 hulls = TRUE, move = FALSE)

title: moveEZ
text: Applying reflection x-axis 1950 restores visual continuity across sequence biplots.
code: bp2 <- bp |> moveplot2(time.var = "Year", group.var = "Region",                 hulls = TRUE, move = FALSE,                 align.time = "1950", reflect = "x")

title: moveEZ
text: moveplot3() automates alignment sequential biplots using GPA (Gower Dijksterhuis 2004), implemented via GPAbin package (Nienkemper-Swanepoel et al. 2023). GPA iteratively applies admissible transformations: translation, reflection, rotation, scaling, minimise sum squared distances time slice target configuration, without requiring user manually identify discontinuities. target argument controls time slices aligned : target = NULL: aligns time slices average (consensus) configuration. target = <dataset>: aligns time slices user-supplied reference dataset containing measurements variables. Africa_climate_target dataset included moveEZ provides 1989 measurements variables Africa_climate, used external reference.

previous_headings: Automated Alignment: moveplot3() > Consensus target (target = NULL)
title: moveEZ
text: time slices aligned average configuration across years, producing consistently oriented sequence biplots without manual intervention.
code: bp |> moveplot3(time.var = "Year", group.var = "Region",                 hulls = TRUE, move = FALSE, target = NULL) #> Object of class biplot, based on 960 samples and 9 variables. #> 6 numeric variables. #> 3 categorical variables.

previous_headings: Automated Alignment: moveplot3() > User-supplied target (target = Africa_climate_target)
title: moveEZ
text: time slice aligned 1989 reference configuration, exposing structural differences 1989 decade 1950 2020. Note target biplot shown display, serves alignment reference. visualise target configuration separately, pass moveplot() directly:
code: bp |> moveplot3(time.var = "Year", group.var = "Region",                 hulls = TRUE, move = FALSE,                 target = Africa_climate_target) #> Object of class biplot, based on 960 samples and 9 variables. #> 6 numeric variables. #> 3 categorical variables. viz_1989 <- Africa_climate_target |>   dplyr::mutate(     Target = as.factor(rep("1989", nrow(Africa_climate_target))),     Region = as.factor(Region)   )  bp_1989 <- biplot(viz_1989, scaled = TRUE) |>   PCA(group.aes = viz_1989$Region)  bp_1989 |> moveplot(time.var = "Target", group.var = "Region",                     hulls = TRUE, move = FALSE) #> Object of class biplot, based on 120 samples and 10 variables. #> 6 numeric variables. #> 4 categorical variables.

title: moveEZ
text: evaluation() function quantifies magnitude structural change time slice target configuration specified moveplot3(). provides five measures based orthogonal Procrustes analysis, two categories: Fit measures (values closer optimal indicate better similarity): PS (Procrustes Statistic): optimal value 0. CC (Congruence Coefficient): optimal value 1. Bias measures (lower values indicate less systematic distortion): AMB (Absolute Mean Bias) MB (Mean Bias): value near zero indicates systematic directional shift. RMSB (Root Mean Squared Bias)
code: results <- bp |>   moveplot3(time.var = "Year", group.var = "Region",             hulls = TRUE, move = FALSE, target = NULL) |>   evaluation()

title: moveEZ
text: Measures comparison
code: results$eval.tab

title: moveEZ
text: biplot 2000 shows notably lower CC higher PS relative years, indicating structural departure consensus configuration warrants closer investigation.
code: results$fit.plot

title: moveEZ
text: initial bias 1950 high decreases stabilises 1960, increase AMB RMSB 2000 consistent fit measures. MB remains close zero throughout, confirming systematic directional bias alignment.
code: results$bias.plot

title: moveEZ
text: group variable can specified time variable produce faceted display panel shows single group rather single time level. can useful group-level patterns difficult distinguish standard faceted display, groups appear together panel.
code: bp |> moveplot(time.var = "Region", group.var = "Region",                hulls = FALSE, move = FALSE) #> Object of class biplot, based on 960 samples and 9 variables. #> 6 numeric variables. #> 3 categorical variables.

title: moveEZ
text: moveEZ inherits core biplot construction biplotEZ, aesthetic customisation, point colours, plotting characters, axis label sizes — specified biplotEZ biplot object passing moveplot function. aesthetic changes made biplotEZ::samples() biplotEZ::axes(), default moveEZ aesthetics applied automatically. One important conversion aware : biplotEZ uses R base graphics sizing, moveEZ renders using ggplot2. Text size therefore automatically rescaled — example, biplotEZ::axes(label.cex = 1) produces ggplot2 text size 2 (.e. geom_text(size = 2)). Adjust label.cex accordingly achieve desired label size final animation.

what: Custom colour palette and axis label size
title: moveEZ
code: bp_custom <- biplotEZ::biplot(Africa_climate, scaled = TRUE,                                group.aes = Africa_climate$Region) |>   biplotEZ::PCA() |>   biplotEZ::samples(col = RColorBrewer::brewer.pal(10, "Paired")) |>   biplotEZ::axes(label.cex = 1.2)  bp_custom |> moveplot(time.var = "Year", group.var = "Region",                       hulls = TRUE, move = FALSE) #> Object of class biplot, based on 960 samples and 9 variables. #> 6 numeric variables. #> 3 categorical variables.

title: moveEZ
text: Note pch values cycle across ten region groups - specify ten values assign unique character region.
code: bp_pch <- biplotEZ::biplot(Africa_climate, scaled = TRUE,                             group.aes = Africa_climate$Region) |>   biplotEZ::PCA() |>   biplotEZ::samples(pch = c(22, 21, 24, 23), opacity = 0.4)  bp_pch |> moveplot(time.var = "Year", group.var = "Region",                    hulls = FALSE, move = FALSE) #> Object of class biplot, based on 960 samples and 9 variables. #> 6 numeric variables. #> 3 categorical variables.

title: Animated Biplots
text: moveEZ (pronounced move easy) extends biplotEZ package animate PCA biplots across ordered levels categorical variable. Rather producing separate static biplot per level, fragments sequential information makes gradual structural change difficult perceive, moveEZ renders transitions levels continuous animation.

title: Animated Biplots
text: Install released version CRAN: install development version GitHub:
code: install.packages("moveEZ") # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("MuViSU/moveEZ")

title: Animated Biplots
text: moveEZ animates PCA biplots across ordered levels categorical variable — referred time variable. variable need represent chronological time; may ordered index experimental stages, algorithmic iterations, measurement occasions. distinct level time variable defines time slice: subset observations corresponding level. package provides three animation functions increasing methodological complexity: three functions support animated output (move = TRUE) static faceted output (move = FALSE).

title: Animated Biplots
code: library(moveEZ) library(biplotEZ)  data("Africa_climate")  bp <- biplot(Africa_climate, scaled = TRUE) |>   PCA(group.aes = Africa_climate$Region) |>   samples(opacity = 0.8) |>   plot()  # Static faceted display bp |> moveplot(time.var = "Year", group.var = "Region",                hulls = TRUE, move = FALSE)  # Animated display bp |> moveplot(time.var = "Year", group.var = "Region",                hulls = TRUE, move = TRUE)

title: Animated Biplots
text: Vignette: full applied demonstration three functions, including animated outputs, evaluation measures, aesthetic customisation - vignette("moveEZ") Paper: accompanying R Journal article provides full methodological treatment, including theoretical motivation framework discussion sign indeterminacy sequential PCA biplots.

what: Measures of comparison for move plot 3 — evaluation
title: Measures of comparison for move plot 3 — evaluation
text: function calculates measures comparison generalised orthogonal Procrustes Analysis performed moveplot3. Orthogonal Procrustes Analysis used compare target testee configuration. following measures calculate: Procrustes Statistic (PS), Congruence Coefficient (CC), Absolute Mean Bias (AMB), Mean Bias (MB) Root Mean Squared Bias (RMSB).

title: Measures of comparison for move plot 3 — evaluation
code: evaluation(bp, centring = TRUE)

title: Measures of comparison for move plot 3 — evaluation
text: bp biplot object moveEZ centring logical argument apply centring (default TRUE)

title: Measures of comparison for move plot 3 — evaluation
text: eval.tab Returns table measures comparison level time variable compared target. fit.plot Returns line plot fit measures bounded zero one: PS CC. small PS value large CC value indicate good fit. bias.plot Returns line plot bias measures unbounded: AMB, MB RMSB. Small values indicate low bias.

title: Measures of comparison for move plot 3 — evaluation
code: data(Africa_climate) data(Africa_climate_target) bp <- biplotEZ::biplot(Africa_climate, scaled = TRUE) |> biplotEZ::PCA() results <- bp |> moveplot3(time.var = "Year", group.var = "Region", hulls = TRUE, move = FALSE, target = NULL) |> evaluation()  results$eval.tab #>  #>  #> Table: Measures of comparison #>  #> |                |  PS   |  CC   |  AMB  | MB | RMSB  | #> |:---------------|:-----:|:-----:|:-----:|:--:|:-----:| #> |Target vs. 1950 | 0.132 | 0.970 | 1.272 | 0  | 1.851 | #> |Target vs. 1960 | 0.098 | 0.976 | 0.441 | 0  | 0.578 | #> |Target vs. 1970 | 0.092 | 0.980 | 0.437 | 0  | 0.570 | #> |Target vs. 1980 | 0.077 | 0.981 | 0.390 | 0  | 0.550 | #> |Target vs. 1990 | 0.081 | 0.979 | 0.418 | 0  | 0.545 | #> |Target vs. 2000 | 0.160 | 0.964 | 0.526 | 0  | 0.656 | #> |Target vs. 2010 | 0.080 | 0.981 | 0.434 | 0  | 0.543 | #> |Target vs. 2020 | 0.069 | 0.981 | 0.391 | 0  | 0.507 | results$fit.plot  results$bias.plot   data(Africa_climate) data(Africa_climate_target) bp <- biplotEZ::biplot(Africa_climate, scaled = TRUE) |> biplotEZ::PCA() results <- bp |> moveplot3(time.var = "Year", group.var = "Region", hulls = TRUE, move = FALSE, target = Africa_climate_target) |> evaluation()  results$eval.tab #>  #>  #> Table: Measures of comparison #>  #> |                |  PS   |  CC   |  AMB  | MB | RMSB  | #> |:---------------|:-----:|:-----:|:-----:|:--:|:-----:| #> |Target vs. 1950 | 0.211 | 0.956 | 0.498 | 0  | 0.655 | #> |Target vs. 1960 | 0.174 | 0.956 | 1.629 | 0  | 2.337 | #> |Target vs. 1970 | 0.205 | 0.952 | 1.647 | 0  | 2.345 | #> |Target vs. 1980 | 0.157 | 0.960 | 1.582 | 0  | 2.319 | #> |Target vs. 1990 | 0.170 | 0.960 | 1.625 | 0  | 2.332 | #> |Target vs. 2000 | 0.247 | 0.945 | 1.698 | 0  | 2.349 | #> |Target vs. 2010 | 0.162 | 0.964 | 1.603 | 0  | 2.318 | #> |Target vs. 2020 | 0.128 | 0.971 | 1.578 | 0  | 2.283 | results$fit.plot  results$bias.plot

One FALSE (default), "x" reflection x-axis, "y" reflection y-axis "xy" reflection axes.

title: Move plot 3 — moveplot3
code: data(Africa_climate) data(Africa_climate_target) bp <- biplot(Africa_climate, scaled = TRUE) |> PCA() bp |> moveplot3(time.var = "Year", group.var = "Region", hulls = TRUE, move = FALSE, target = NULL)  #> Object of class biplot, based on 960 samples and 9 variables. #> 6 numeric variables. #> 3 categorical variables. # \donttest{ if(interactive()) { bp |> moveplot3(time.var = "Year", group.var = "Region", hulls = TRUE, move = TRUE, target = NULL)}# } bp |> moveplot3(time.var = "Year", group.var = "Region", hulls = TRUE, move = FALSE, target = Africa_climate_target)  #> Object of class biplot, based on 960 samples and 9 variables. #> 6 numeric variables. #> 3 categorical variables. data(Africa_climate) data(Africa_climate_target) bp <- biplot(Africa_climate, scaled = TRUE) |> PCA() bp |> moveplot3(time.var = "Year", group.var = "Region", hulls = TRUE, move = FALSE, target = NULL)  #> Object of class biplot, based on 960 samples and 9 variables. #> 6 numeric variables. #> 3 categorical variables. # \donttest{ if(interactive()) { bp |> moveplot3(time.var = "Year", group.var = "Region", hulls = TRUE, move = TRUE, target = NULL)}# } bp |> moveplot3(time.var = "Year", group.var = "Region", hulls = TRUE, move = FALSE, target = Africa_climate_target)  #> Object of class biplot, based on 960 samples and 9 variables. #> 6 numeric variables. #> 3 categorical variables.
